subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Locations
- **1** main game board (Viking village with worker placement spaces)
- **Ship tiles** — used to carry warriors on sea journeys
- **Monster cards** — Troll cards, Draugr cards, Journey (sea) monster cards

#### Dice (Warriors)
- **Swordsmen dice** (red) — balanced attack and defense
- **Spearmen dice** (yellow) — higher attack, lower defense
- **Axemen dice** (black) — highest attack, no defense
- Each die face shows: **sword** (hit), **shield** (defend), **blank** (miss), or **sword+shield** (hit and defend)

#### Resources & Tokens
- **Food tokens** — provisions for sea journeys
- **Wood tokens** — used for ships and buildings
- **Gold tokens** — currency and VP
- **Coins** — currency
- **Favour of the Gods tokens** — allow dice re-rolls
- **Blame tokens** — negative VP for unfought monsters
- **Glory (VP) tokens**

#### Player Components
- **3 workers** per player (Viking meeples)
- **1 Leader card** per player (unique ability)
- **Score markers**

#### Other
- **Rune cards** — provide special abilities
- **Destiny cards** — events during sea journeys

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Place the main board** — Shows the Viking village with multiple worker spaces.
2. **Village locations** — Blacksmith, Swordsmith, Spearmaker, Market, Shipwright, Sage, Jarl's Longhouse, Stave Church, and more.
3. **Troll space** — Place a troll card face-up. One troll threatens the village each round.
4. **Draugr spaces** — Place draugr cards on the journey preparation area.
5. **Journey locations** — Distant lands where sea monsters await. Place journey monster cards.
6. **Ship dock** — Place available ship tiles.
7. **Rune card display** — Lay out available rune cards.
8. **Supply area** — Organize dice by color, food, wood, gold, coins, and tokens.
9. **Blame token pile** — Place near the troll space.
10. **Destiny deck** — Shuffle and place face-down near the journey area.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player Receives
- **3 Viking workers** in their color
- **1 Leader card** — draft or deal randomly. Each leader has a unique ongoing ability.
- **Starting resources** — a small amount of coins, food, and possibly 1 warrior die (varies by turn order)
- **1 score marker** — place on 0 of Glory track

#### Leader Cards
- Each leader provides a different advantage: extra dice in certain combats, bonus resources, Glory bonuses, etc.
- Leaders create asymmetry between players.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **8 rounds** — The game is played over 8 rounds.
- **Each round** — All players place workers, resolve combats, then clean up.
- **First player** — Determined randomly, passes clockwise each round.
- **Troll appears** — A new troll card is revealed each round threatening the village.
- **Journey monsters** — New journey monsters appear based on round.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
1. **Reveal Monsters** — Flip a new troll card and any new journey/draugr cards.
2. **Worker Placement Phase** — Starting player places 1 worker, then clockwise. Continue until all workers are placed.
3. **Combat Phase** — Resolve all combats (troll, draugr, journey monsters) simultaneously.
4. **Clean Up** — Return workers, advance round marker, pass first player.

#### Worker Placement
- Place **1 worker at a time**, alternating with other players.
- Each space holds **1 worker** (most spaces are exclusive).
- 3 workers per player means 3 placement turns per round.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Resource Gathering Spaces
- **Blacksmith** — Gain swordsmen dice (red warriors).
- **Swordsmith** — Gain swordsmen dice.
- **Spearmaker** — Gain spearmen dice (yellow warriors).
- **Market** — Buy or sell resources and food.
- **Hunting Lodge** — Gain food for journeys.
- **Goldsmith** — Gain gold.

#### Preparation Spaces
- **Shipwright** — Gain a ship tile (required for sea journeys).
- **Sage** — Gain a rune card (special ability).
- **Stave Church** — Gain Favour of the Gods tokens (dice re-rolls).

#### Combat Spaces
- **Troll Attack** — Assign warriors to fight the troll threatening the village. If no one fights, ALL players gain a **blame token** (-3 Glory).
- **Draugr Hunt** — Assign warriors and a ship to fight draugr at sea.
- **Journey** — Assign warriors, food, and a ship to sail to distant lands and fight journey monsters.

#### Special Spaces
- **Jarl's Longhouse** — Become first player next round and gain a bonus.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Dice Combat
- Assign warrior dice to a monster. Roll all assigned dice.
- **Sword faces** = hits (deal damage to monster).
- **Shield faces** = defense (absorb monster's damage).
- **Blank faces** = miss.
- **Sword+Shield** = both hit and defend.
- Monster card shows its **attack value** and **health**.
- You must deal damage equal to monster's health to defeat it.
- Monster deals its attack value back — undefended damage **kills your warrior dice** (remove them from the game).

#### Favour of the Gods
- Spend Favour tokens to **re-roll** any number of your dice during combat.
- Extremely valuable for close fights.

#### Sea Journeys
- To go on a journey, you need a **ship** and enough **food** to feed your warriors.
- Draw **Destiny cards** during the voyage — these can be positive (bonus resources) or negative (storms that kill warriors).
- Journey monsters are the most valuable to defeat.

#### Blame Tokens
- If the troll is **not defeated** by any player, ALL players receive a blame token.
- Each blame token is **-3 Glory** at end of game.
- This creates shared pressure to fight the troll.

#### Monster Sets
- Defeating different **types** of monsters (troll, draugr, journey) scores bonus Glory for collecting sets.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End of Game
After **8 rounds**, the game ends.

#### Final Scoring
- **Defeated Monsters** — Glory printed on each defeated monster card.
- **Monster Sets** — Bonus Glory for sets of different monster types (1 of each type = bonus).
- **Gold** — Each gold token is worth Glory.
- **Coins** — Remaining coins convert to Glory.
- **Blame Tokens** — Each is **-3 Glory**.
- **Leader Bonuses** — Some leaders score bonus Glory for specific conditions.
- **Rune Card Bonuses** — Some rune cards have end-game Glory.

#### Tiebreaker
Player with more remaining warrior dice wins ties.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Collect warrior dice** — You need dice to fight. Prioritize gaining swordsmen or spearmen early.
- **Get a ship** — Without a ship you cannot attempt sea journeys, which are the most rewarding.
- **Fight the troll** — The blame penalty affects everyone, so someone must fight. If you can win cheaply, do it for easy Glory.
- **Stock food** — Sea journeys require food. Running out means you can't go on profitable voyages.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring the troll** — Blame tokens add up fast. -3 per token over 8 rounds can be -24 Glory.
- **Sending too few dice** — Losing warriors to failed combats is devastating since dice are permanently lost.
- **No ships** — Without ships, you miss the most valuable journey combats.
- **Hoarding Favour tokens** — Use them when the fight is close. Don't save them all for the end.
- **Neglecting monster set bonuses** — Try to defeat at least one of each type for the set bonus.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Warrior type** — Axemen (black) deal maximum damage but can't defend. Swordsmen (red) are balanced. Spearmen (yellow) lean offensive.
- **Troll duty** — Who fights the troll? It's tempting to let others handle it, but blame affects everyone.
- **Journey risk** — Destiny cards add uncertainty. More food and warriors reduce risk but cost more.
- **Leader synergy** — Play to your leader's strength. If your leader boosts sea combat, prioritize journeys.
- **Timing big fights** — Save your best warriors for high-value monsters in later rounds.
